name: entry_base_collision
code:
proof fn phi_1_entry_base_collision(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < arch.num_entries(layer),
        j < arch.num_entries(layer),
        i != j,
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, j),
{
}
reason: Different indices should map to distinct base addresses; collision would allow overlapping memory regions

name: arch_inv_allows_zero_layers
code:
proof fn phi_2_arch_inv_allows_zero_layers(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
    ensures
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: A valid architecture with zero layers is degenerate and maps no address space, suggesting inv is too permissive

name: interp_maps_below_base
code:
proof fn phi_3_interp_maps_below_base(d: Directory, base: nat, pte: PTE)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        d.interp().contains_pair(base, pte),
    ensures
        base < d.base_vaddr,
{
}
reason: Interpreted mappings should only cover addresses at or above the directory's base_vaddr; mapping below would be unsound

name: page_zero_frame_size
code:
proof fn phi_4_page_zero_frame_size(d: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Page,
    ensures
        d.entries[i as int]->Page_0.frame.size == 0nat,
{
}
reason: A page frame with zero size under a valid invariant means the page maps no memory, which defeats the purpose of the mapping

name: well_formed_exceeds_max_entries
code:
proof fn phi_5_well_formed_exceeds_max_entries(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.well_formed(),
    ensures
        d.num_entries() > X86_NUM_ENTRIES,
{
}
reason: A well-formed directory should not have more entries than the x86 architectural maximum of 512
